title: Tool-selection audit round 2: moment-grouped grounding, task-phrase context fallback, SessionStart orientation, actionable capture nudge
context: Second agent-perspective audit, focused on WHEN tools get used, not what data exists. Honest usage measurement: 4 of 19 hunch tools used across a 3-day feature build. Root causes found: (1) hunch_context — the task-shaped entry point — returned EMPTY for task phrases ('improve retrieval ranking' whiffed while the graph held that exact roadmap decision one FTS query away); (2) the CLAUDE.md auto block advertised 9 of 19 tools, moment-less, omitting hunch_structure and hunch_context entirely; (3) no orientation at session start — the sit-down moment pulled nothing from the graph; (4) the capture-interview nudge was passive and ignored 12/12 times.
decision: Four fixes. (1) hunch_context (CLI + MCP) falls back to FTS when the target resolves to no file/symbol — a task phrase now returns the closest graph matches instead of a shrug; tool description says so. (2) renderHunchSection rewritten MOMENT-grouped — Orient / Before designing / Before editing / Before committing / After deciding — covering the full tool surface incl. structure, context, current_decision, pr_impact, merge_verdict, record_correction, timeline, and `hunch now`; agents trigger-match on moments, not tool nouns. (3) SessionStart hook support: `hunch hook` handles the SessionStart event with a cheap public-store orientation (recent decisions, live roadmap, pending-review count) — scaffolded into new installs, added to this repo's local settings. (4) The un-token'd record_decision nudge now embeds the FIRST grilling question inline with the record's own title, so hardening a quick record costs one exchange instead of a flow switch.
consequences: Existing repos pick up the new grounding block + SessionStart entry on their next `hunch init` / grounding refresh; this machine's global hunch handles SessionStart only after the next release + npm -g update
The moment list adds ~15 lines to every grounding doc — paid once per doc, offset many times over by the injection dedup
Roadmap items that remain deliberately open: retrieval RANKING quality (dec_25e277f479) and wiki/now over MCP (dec_29eff08c69) — this round fixed reachability, not relevance ordering
alternatives_rejected: Semantic fallback inside store.assembleContext: src/store/** edits are gate-heavy and the store layer should stay target-shaped; the fallback belongs at the presentation layer (CLI/MCP) where emptiness is judged
Advertising every tool in one flat list: 19 bullets with no WHEN is how 4/19 usage happens; the moment grouping IS the fix
SessionStart running computeDrift for a fuller picture: drift walks all markdown + wiki hashing — too slow for a hook that fires on every session; orientation must be loadAll-cheap
Forcing capture tokens now (rejecting un-token'd writes): breaks the staged-deprecation contract of dec capture-token; the inline-question nudge moves behavior without breaking writes
